# SCULPTURE OF ANGKOR AND ANCIENT CAMBODIA: Millennium of Glory
Jessup, Helen Ibbitson and Thierry Zephir New York:Thames & Hudson,1997.First Edition.Hardcover. "This richly illustrated volume, published to coincide with an exhibition organized by the National Gallery of Art and the Reunion des Musees Nationnaux, examines the powerful and original Khmer culture that flourished on the mainland of Southeast Asia between 600 and 1600 A.D." Quarto: 381 p. with 256 illustrations, 153 in color. Original tan

title: Inside Ideas: Helen Ibbitson Jessup on Khmer Rouge & Iraq

June 4, 2010 The International Festival of Arts & Ideas is filled with great Ideas panels (which are totally FREE). Helen Ibbitson Jessup, founder of Friends of Khmer Culture and specialist in the art and architecture of Southeast Asia and Cambodia, is a panelist in one of our upcoming events.
